primary_accounting_issue: Eligibility of intragroup dividend receivables as hedged items in a hedge accounting relationship for foreign exchange risk in consolidated financial statements

primary_authority.reason: Defines eligible hedged items and establishes the general requirement that hedged items must involve parties external to the reporting entity, while providing an exception for foreign currency risk on intragroup monetary items

supporting_authority.reason: Provides specific guidance on hedge accounting for net investments in foreign operations and clarifies the scope of foreign currency risks that qualify as hedged risks in consolidated financial statements

peripheral_authority.reason: Governs foreign currency translation and exchange differences but does not specifically address hedge accounting eligibility for intragroup items
Addresses consolidation principles but does not provide specific guidance on hedge accounting for intragroup foreign exchange exposures
Addresses financial instrument classification and presentation but is not directly relevant to hedge accounting eligibility

treatment_families.family: General restriction on hedging intragroup items vs. exception for foreign currency risk on intragroup monetary items
Hedge accounting for foreign currency risk arising from net investment in foreign operation

approaches.label: Hedge accounting prohibited for intragroup dividend receivable
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: Under IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.5, hedged items must involve parties external to the reporting entity; intragroup dividend receivables are internal transactions and cannot be designated as hedged items absent a specific exception

approaches.label: Hedge accounting permitted under the intragroup monetary item exception
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.6 provides an explicit exception permitting foreign currency risk of intragroup monetary items to qualify as a hedged item in consolidated financial statements when exchange differences are not fully eliminated on consolidation

approaches.label: Hedge accounting as net investment hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRIC 16 clarifies that hedge accounting may apply to foreign currency risk arising from net investments in foreign operations; if the dividend receivable relates to net assets of a foreign subsidiary, it may qualify under this framework
